heading: Enhancing the ability of State, local, and Tribal child welfare agencies to identify and respond to children who are, or are at risk of being, victims of trafficking
label: 20705a Enhancing the ability of State, local, and Tribal child welfare agencies to identify and respond to children who are, or are at risk of being, victims of trafficking

children.heading: Grants to enhance child welfare services
children.label: a Grants to enhance child welfare services

children.text: children considered victims of “child abuse and neglect” and of “sexual abuse” under the application of because of being identified as being a victim or at risk of being a victim of a severe form of trafficking in persons; andsection 5106g(b)(1) of title 42

children over whom such agencies have responsibility for placement, care, or supervision and for whom there is reasonable cause to believe are, or are at risk of being a victim of 1 or more severe forms of trafficking in persons.

children.heading: Child
children.label: 1 Child
children.text: The term “child” means an individual who has not attained 18 years of age or such older age as the State has elected under section 475(8) of the Social Security Act (). At the option of an eligible State, such term may include an individual who has not attained 26 years of age.42 U.S.C. 675(8)

children.heading: Elimination of third party control requirement
children.label: A Elimination of third party control requirement
children.text: The State has eliminated or will eliminate any requirement relating to identification of a controlling third party who causes a child to engage in a commercial sex act in order for the child to be considered a victim of trafficking or a victim of 1 or more severe forms of trafficking in persons for purposes of accessing child welfare services and care.

children.heading: Application of standard for human trafficking
children.label: B Application of standard for human trafficking
children.text: The State considers a child to be a victim of trafficking if the individual is a victim of a severe form of trafficking in persons, as described in subparagraph (A) of .section 7102(11) of title 22

children.heading: Development and implementation of State child welfare plan protocols
children.label: C Development and implementation of State child welfare plan protocols
i
children.text: The requirement to report immediately, and in no case later than 24 hours after receiving, information on children who have been identified as being a victim of a severe form of trafficking in persons to law enforcement authorities under paragraph (34)(A) of section 471(a) of the Social Security Act ().42 U.S.C. 671(a)

children.label: ii
children.text: The requirement to report immediately, and in no case later than 24 hours after receiving, information on missing or abducted children to law enforcement authorities, including children classified as “runaways”, for entry into the National Crime Information Center (NCIC) database of the Federal Bureau of Investigation, and to the National Center for Missing and Exploited Children, under paragraph (35)(B) of such section [].42 U.S.C. 671(a)(35)(B)

children.label: iii
children.text: The requirement to report to the Secretary of Health and Human Services the total number of children who are victims of child human trafficking under paragraph (34)(B) of such section [].42 U.S.C. 671(a)(34)(B)

children.heading: Trafficking-specific protocol
children.label: D Trafficking-specific protocol
children.text: The State has developed and implemented or will develop and implement a specialized protocol for responding to a child who is, or is at risk of being, a trafficking victim to ensure the response focuses on the child’s specific safety needs as a victim of trafficking, and that includes the development and use of an alternative mechanism for investigating and responding to cases of child human trafficking in which the alleged offender is not the child’s parent or caregiver without utilizing existing processes for investigating and responding to other forms of child abuse or neglect that require the filing of an abuse or neglect petition.

children.heading: Indian tribe; tribal organization
children.label: 3 Indian tribe; tribal organization
children.text: The term “Indian tribe” and “tribal organization” have the meanings given those terms in .section 5304 of title 25

children.heading: State
children.label: 4 State
children.text: The term “State” means each of the 50 States, the District of Columbia, the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico, the United States Virgin Islands, Guam, American Samoa, and the Commonwealth of the Northern Mariana Islands. Such term includes an Indian tribe, tribal organization, or tribal consortium with a plan approved under section 479B of the Social Security Act (), or which is receiving funding to provide foster care under part E of title IV of such Act [ et seq.] pursuant to a cooperative agreement or contract with a State.42 U.S.C. 679c42 U.S.C. 670
